issues.title: [PLATFORM] Add dry-run guard + pre-write backup to wp_post_ia_rollout.py
issues.body:
## Problem

`scripts/wp_post_ia_rollout.py` (~671 LOC) performs live WordPress writes (post meta, featured images, media reorder) but has **no `--dry-run` default and no pre-write backup**. A mistaken run mutates production immediately, against the post-INCIDENT-2026-05-15 safety posture used everywhere else in the publisher.

## Impact

- **CRITICAL safety** - the one live-write script that doesn't follow the snapshot-first / dry-run-first convention.
- Risk of an unrecoverable bulk metadata/media mutation on kriskrug.co.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--dry-run` is the DEFAULT; writes only happen with explicit `--execute`
- [ ] Dry-run prints every intended write (post id, field, old -> new) and makes zero REST writes
- [ ] Before any live mutate, snapshot the target objects to `backup/<ts>/`
- [ ] Hardcoded environment paths/IDs surfaced as args or config
- [ ] A smoke test (or unit test) confirms dry-run makes no network writes

## Notes

Mirror the guard pattern already in `scripts/notion-to-wp/kk_notion_to_wp.py` / `update_safety.py`.

issues.title: [SEO] Route one-off publish scripts through normalize_seo_meta (completes #248)
issues.body:
## Problem

#248 added `normalize_seo_meta()` in `scripts/notion-to-wp/connector_payload.py` to stop Jetpack SEO REST 500s on combining diacritics. But the three one-off publish scripts set `jetpack_seo_html_title` / `advanced_seo_description` **directly**, bypassing the fix: `publish_dc_protest_draft.py`, `publish_you_cant_drink_data.py`, `publish_proximity_game.py`. A combining-diacritic title (e.g. "Ethọ́s") in any of these still 500s.

## Impact

- **Platform reliability** - the diacritic fix is incomplete; future one-off publishes hit the same wall.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All three one-off scripts route SEO title + description through `normalize_seo_meta` (import the helper, or refactor to `build_wp_payload`)
- [ ] Visible title/body diacritics untouched; only the SEO-meta fields normalized
- [ ] A test exercises a combining-diacritic title through the shared path and asserts no combining marks remain

## Notes

Refs #248. Reuse `connector_payload.normalize_seo_meta`; do not duplicate the logic.

issues.title: [REFACTOR] Shared scripts/common.py for env loading + WP REST client
issues.body:
## Problem

The ~11 root-level scripts in `scripts/` each re-implement environment loading (`.env` cascade) and a WordPress REST client (auth headers, session, endpoints). `scripts/notion-to-wp/wp_client.py` is not shared with them. This is the largest duplication surface in the repo.

## Impact

- **Maintainability** - auth/endpoint/env changes must be made in many places; drift risk.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scripts/common.py` exposes a cascade env loader and a shared WP client
- [ ] Root scripts import from it instead of re-implementing
- [ ] Existing read-only audits still pass (`make seo-audit`, `make current-state-drift-check`, etc.)
- [ ] No behavior change to existing commands

## Notes

Reuse/alias `scripts/notion-to-wp/wp_client.py` where possible.

issues.title: [REFACTOR] Consolidate one-off publish_*.py image/block/link logic + de-hardcode IDs
issues.body:
## Problem

`publish_dc_protest_draft.py`, `publish_you_cant_drink_data.py`, `publish_proximity_game.py` each reimplement image upload, Gutenberg block assembly, and markdown/link rendering, and carry hardcoded IDs (e.g. `CATEGORY_ID=1678`, `FEATURED_ID`). Bug fixes (em-dash handling, alt text) must be replicated three times.

## Impact

- **Maintainability** - triplicated logic; hardcoded prod IDs are unsafe if reused against staging.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shared utilities extracted to `scripts/notion-to-wp/publish_common.py` (image manifest, block builder, link renderer)
- [ ] The three scripts import the shared utilities
- [ ] Hardcoded category/featured IDs moved to args/config with existence validation
- [ ] Publisher test suite stays green

## Notes

Depends loosely on the normalize_seo_meta routing (sibling issue). Consider whether these can eventually be parameterized `kk_notion_to_wp.py` runs.

issues.title: [TESTS] Add CI unit tests for critical root scripts
issues.body:
## Problem

The publisher (`scripts/notion-to-wp/`) has solid unit tests in CI, but the root-level scripts — notably `wp_post_ia_rollout.py` (live writes) and `public_image_audit.py` — have zero unit coverage. CI (`.github/workflows/test-pr.yml`) runs publisher tests + PHPCS + docs-truth + Trivy but never exercises these.

## Impact

- **Reliability** - the riskiest root script is the least tested.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit tests for `wp_post_ia_rollout.py` (esp. dry-run makes no writes) and `public_image_audit.py` core logic
- [ ] Tests wired into `test-pr.yml`
- [ ] CI green

## Notes

Pairs with the dry-run-guard issue; the dry-run test belongs here.

issues.title: [OPS] CSS dead-code audit + clarify canonical schema snippet
issues.body:
## Problem

Two small hygiene items from the engineering scan:
1. `theme/kk-aurora/style.css` is ~4,053 LOC / ~95KB with unclear dead selectors (gradient/glow utilities of unknown usage).
2. `fixes/schema-snippets.php` vs `fixes/schema-snippets-deployed.php` — which is canonical is ambiguous; the deployed one has a TODO about a missing headshot URL.

## Impact

- **Tech debt / clarity** - low severity, but easy wins for maintainability and payload size.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Run a CSS coverage audit; remove or document intentionally-unused selectors
- [ ] Add a header comment marking `schema-snippets-deployed.php` canonical and `schema-snippets.php` reference/archive
- [ ] Resolve or ticket the headshot-URL TODO in the deployed schema snippet

## Notes

Track B for the CSS portion; do not change visible rendering without a theme deploy.
